title: Remote/local routing: local fallback when no Pod + 'Auto-connect on start' checkbox
description: UX/routing fix found live 2026-06-15 (MPI-64 mask test). When 'Enable RunPod remote engine' is ON but NO Pod is connected (app start with no/unavailable Pod, OR a mid-session disconnect), every generation hard-fails at _ensureRemoteReady with 'Remote engine not connected' — even though the LOCAL engine is ready (status bar showed LOCAL·OFFLINE, RTX 4060 Ti, 7/7 models). The toggle fuses two concepts: 'remote is available' and 'route ALL gens to remote'. Decisions (user, 2026-06-15): (1) when no Pod is connected, AUTO-FALLBACK generation to the local engine + a one-time info toast (guard remote-only models with a clear 'Connect to use this model' message); (2) add a new 'Automatically connect on app start' checkbox (default OFF) that owns the boot auto-connect lifecycle, decoupled from 'Enable RunPod remote engine' (which becomes 'remote available / show panel', not 'force remote'). Routing follows ACTUAL connection state, not the toggle. See brief.md.
